Shirdi Sai Baba Temple

Sabka Malik Ek  ·  One God for All

Explore
✦   Sacred Abode   ✦

What is the Shirdi Sai Baba Temple?

The Shirdi Sai Baba Temple, formally known as the Samadhi Mandir, is one of the most visited pilgrimage sites in India and a spiritual landmark revered by millions across religions. It is dedicated to Sai Baba of Shirdi, a 19th-century spiritual master who preached the unity of God, love, compassion, and faith — transcending the boundaries of Hinduism and Islam.

The temple complex houses Sai Baba's sacred samadhi (tomb), a magnificent white-marble structure that draws pilgrims from every corner of the world seeking blessings, healing, and solace. The atmosphere within is deeply devotional — incense, devotional songs, and the collective faith of thousands create an overwhelming sense of the divine.

Location & History

Shirdi is a small town in the Ahmednagar district of Maharashtra, India, approximately 296 km from Mumbai and 83 km from Aurangabad. Despite its modest size, it receives over 25,000 to 40,000 pilgrims daily — more on festival days — making it one of the wealthiest religious trusts in India.

Sai Baba first appeared in Shirdi around 1858 as a young fakir and lived there for decades, performing miracles and teaching humanity. He attained Mahasamadhi on 15 October 1918. The Samadhi Mandir was built over his tomb and consecrated in 1922 by Gopalrao Gund.

LocationShirdi, Ahmednagar, Maharashtra, India
Dedicated toSai Baba of Shirdi (c. 1838 – 1918)
Temple Consecrated1922 CE
Managing TrustShri Saibaba Sansthan Trust
Annual Visitors~8–12 million pilgrims
Nearest CityAurangabad (~83 km)

Historical & Spiritual Importance

Sai Baba is unique in the Indian spiritual tradition for being revered equally by Hindus, Muslims, Zoroastrians and people of all faiths. His core teachings — "Shraddha" (faith) and "Saburi" (patience) — remain profoundly relevant today. He is believed to have performed countless miracles: curing the ill, filling lamps with water, and bilocating.

The temple is not merely a religious monument but a symbol of communal harmony. The Dwarkamai mosque where Baba lived, the Chavadi, and the Lendi Baug garden are equally sacred sites within the complex, each narrating a chapter of his life.

"Allah Malik Hai" — God is the Master of All.

— Sai Baba of Shirdi

Interesting Facts

  • Sai Baba lived in a dilapidated mosque he called Dwarkamai for over 60 years.
  • The Samadhi Mandir is constructed entirely of Italian white marble.
  • The Shri Saibaba Sansthan Trust is one of the richest religious trusts in India.
  • Shirdi receives more visitors than the Taj Mahal on most pilgrimage days.
  • Baba is depicted wearing kafni (robe) in the iconic seated posture.
  • His teachings span both Sufi and Vedanta philosophies.
  • The Lendi Baug garden was personally watered by Sai Baba every day.
  • An annual endowment of over ₹350 crore is used for charitable work.
  • Sai Baba never married, never owned property, and subsisted on alms.
  • He is recognised as a saint by numerous Hindu peethas and Sufi orders.

✦   Shirdi Sai Baba Temple  ·  Shirdi, Maharashtra, India   ✦
